Exclude gitignored files from Lambda layer and source code bundles (#7)

* Initial plan

* feat: exclude gitignored files from lambda layer and code bundles

- Add gitignore_exclude_patterns() utility to bundler.py that reads the
  root .gitignore and returns all non-comment, non-blank patterns
- DepsBundler.__init__ now stores patterns; try_bundle passes them to
  shutil.copytree(ignore=...) so __pycache__, *.pyc, .venv/, etc. are
  excluded when copying workspace packages into the layer
- hello_stack.py passes exclude=gitignore_exclude_patterns() with
  ignore_mode=IgnoreMode.GIT to Code.from_asset for the lambda handler,
  keeping gitignored artefacts out of the deployment zip
- Add 5 new tests covering the new function and copytree ignore behaviour

Co-authored-by: klemjul <60812459+klemjul@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/klemjul/python-aws-monorepo-boilerplate/sessions/bbd1571f-8fa5-4142-b027-9555f94766b0

* fix: apply ignore callback at top level of workspace src copy

Replace the manual entry-by-entry loop with a single shutil.copytree call
on src_dir directly, so the ignore callback (built from .gitignore patterns)
is applied at every level including the top level of src/. The old loop
only passed ignore= when recursing into sub-directories, meaning top-level
__pycache__/, *.pyc, and similar entries would still be copied into the layer.

commit.message:
fix(api): add raw body capture for webhook signature verification

Fastify doesn't expose rawBody by default. Override the JSON content-type
parser to store the raw Buffer on the request object. Without this, the
inbound webhook route was computing HMAC signatures from an empty buffer,
causing all webhook signature verifications to fail.

commit.message:
fix(api): use client secret as fallback for webhook signature verification

WHOOP (and potentially other providers) use the OAuth client secret as
the webhook signing key — there is no separate webhook secret. The
inbound route now falls back to <PROVIDER>_CLIENT_SECRET when
<PROVIDER>_WEBHOOK_SECRET is not set.

This means WHOOP webhooks will work out of the box without needing to
set WHOOP_WEBHOOK_SECRET separately — the existing WHOOP_CLIENT_SECRET
is used for HMAC-SHA256 signature verification.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add migration for inbound_webhook_logs table

  models per type (GOBLIN/WOLF/SKELETON/TROLL), loot via spawnLoot event,
  18 s respawn cycle

- NPC3D: robe+hat humanoid, facing/interaction within range, DOM 'Press E
  to talk' hint, merchant gem stand, AethoriaAI dialogue (same as NPC.js)

- Boss3D: extends Enemy3D; VOID_KNIGHT (purple armour, pulsing eye
  PointLight), STONE_COLOSSUS (boulder fists); phase transitions, SLAM
  AoE state, aura PointLight; bosses do not respawn

No Phaser APIs used anywhere; all events via EventBus; THREE imported
from engine/Renderer.js.

fix: stop agent turn loop when issue state stalls

The agent runner now tracks consecutive turns where the Linear issue
state doesn't change. After max_stale_turns (default 3) such turns,
the loop stops early instead of burning through all max_turns.

Also improves the continuation prompt to explicitly instruct the agent
to move the issue to Human Review when work is complete, reducing the
likelihood of stale turns in the first place.

Changes:
- Add agent.max_stale_turns config (default: 3)
- Refactor do_run_codex_turns to use a context map (fixes credo arity)
- Enhance continuation prompt with state transition instructions
- Add test for stale-turn limit behavior

Add World3D renderer and remove Phaser dependency from WorldGen

- Replace Phaser.Math.Between(a, b) in WorldGen.getEnemySpawns() with
  the pure-JS equivalent Math.floor(Math.random()*(b-a+1))+a.

- Add src/world/World3D.js: chunk-based 3D world renderer using Three.js.
  * TILE_DEF table maps all 10 tile IDs to geometry/material properties.
  * InstancedMesh per tile-type per 16×16 chunk for GPU-efficient rendering.
  * Water tiles emit a second transparent PlaneGeometry overlay (depthWrite:false).
  * Deep-water plane sits 0.1 units above seafloor for a depth illusion.
  * Grass tiles use setColorAt() for per-tile colour jitter.
  * Forest tiles get instanced CylinderGeometry trunks + ConeGeometry canopies
    with ±15% random scale and yaw variation.
  * Lazy chunk creation; chunks toggled visible/hidden by updateVisibleChunks().
  * Lighting: AmbientLight + HemisphereLight + shadow-casting DirectionalLight.
  * setTimeOfDay(t) animates sun elevation, colour warmth, ambient and sky.
  * dispose() correctly separates per-chunk InstancedMesh buffer disposal
    from shared geometry/material disposal to avoid double-free.
